"""
Make a naive datetime timezone aware.

Attach timezone information to a naive datetime value and return an aware
datetime in the provided timezone. If timezone is None, TIMEZONE is used.

This function expects a naive datetime; if the value is already timezone-aware
(i.e., localized), a ValueError is raised to avoid overwriting existing tzinfo.

If the datetime has a 'fold' attribute (Python 3.6+), set fold=1 to emulate
the second instance of a clock time during DST backward transitions.

Timezone handling:
- If the tz object has a localize method (as with pytz timezones), use timezone.localize(value)
- If the tz object has a convert method (as with pendulum), use timezone.convert(value)
- Otherwise, fall back to value.replace(tzinfo=timezone) (may be incorrect around DST changes)
"""
